mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a non-inflationary economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of INDPRO accelerating and UNRATE declining fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days because resilient consumer spending extends the earnings expansion before a downturn.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of late-cycle months, or the spread between top and bottom RSAFS quintiles is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding corporate output and revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth does not predict positive 5-day SPX returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates the equity risk premium and compresses valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not negative in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the mean triggered return is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signal a broadening demand contraction that compresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of HOUST and RSAFS both declining does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects aggregate demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity earnings revisions follow output.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to positively predict 20-day SPX returns with a walk-forward Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
